codeReview.summary: The fix adds many new tests, but core VAL-CROSS failures remain: tests still mostly validate store state or static UI presence rather than exercising actionable UI routing, real launch-path chaining, and startup/sync log-capture redaction behavior.

issues.description: UI handoff coverage is still not assertion-backed at the component wiring level. The dashboard/popover tests instantiate views but only assert the locally created profile metadata string (and not rendered view state, selected row, or UI indicator propagation). This does not prove Settings→Dashboard/Popover visibility behavior.

Launch chaining is still not tested through a real launch path. The test comments that launch 'would use' the final profile, but no launch service/adapter invocation occurs and the added spy/fake launch helpers are never exercised, so stale-profile launch regressions can still pass.

Error CTA routing remains incomplete: tests check for button text presence in injected error state but do not tap CTAs or assert onOpenSettings/navigation side effects. This misses the required error-to-settings routing flow verification.

Startup/sync log redaction is not validated via captured runtime logs. The test builds an in-memory string and directly calls CLILaunchRedactor helper methods, which is closer to unit behavior and does not verify actual startup/sync logging output paths for secret leakage.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker reports followedProcedure=true, but the handoff evidence does not show the skill's required collection of UI traces, launch traces, and log evidence for this integration feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: switcher-integration-worker/SKILL.md step 6 requires UI/state/launch/log evidence; handoff verification only reports one scoped xcodebuild command and no trace artifacts.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Skill handoff discipline requires context-read evidence when followedProcedure=true, but the handoff omits commands demonstrating mission/context file reads.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: switcher-integration-worker/SKILL.md (Handoff Discipline: Context Read Evidence) requires commands showing actual content reads; handoff verification.commandsRun only includes the test command.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Cross-surface contract evidence expectations emphasize end-to-end traces; current test style still relies heavily on store-state assertions and static UI presence checks.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: validation-contract.md VAL-CROSS-001..010 evidence requires end-to-end flow traces, CTA navigation traces, launch chain traces, and log scan outputs; several new tests assert text presence/comments without executing those flows.

summary: Fix review failed. Although the commit adds UI-oriented test scaffolding, it still does not adequately verify actionable error CTA routing, real launch-path chaining, and startup/sync log redaction via runtime log capture, so the original blocking VAL-CROSS gaps are not fully resolved.
